schema_version.description: Payload schema_version. 1.0.0 = legacy ungrounded; 1.1.0 = Phase Y grounded (PR #128 source_turns contract — unchanged here); 1.2.0 = additionally permits the optional rationale (decisions) / claims with external_references + evidence_in_transcript / follow_up_required (action_items) / word_level_timestamps / sentiment_indicators / meeting_phases fields; 1.3.0 = additionally permits eight new optional cross-meeting arrays (issue_registry_entry, position_statement, dissent_or_objection, agenda_item, precedent_reference, external_stakeholder_input, glossary_definition, procedural_ruling) plus the optional claim_complexity field on claims items. Additivity: every 1.2.0 AND 1.3.0 field is optional, so a legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 / 1.2.0 artifact validates unchanged on the 1.3.0 schema, and an artifact may declare 1.3.0 without carrying any new field.

decisions.description: Additive (schema-version-agnostic): an item may be a legacy verbatim decision STRING or a structured object. The string branch keeps every existing regex / LLM artifact and the legacy LLM strict-schema path valid. The object branch carries the canonical typed-decision shape (`text`, optional `verb`) plus the two optional architecture-review fields `stakeholders` and `confidence`. `text`/`verb` match what evals/regulatory_verb.py reads so an object-form decision is still verb-classified, not silently bypassed.

stakeholders.description: Optional, default []. Names of stakeholders affected by or responsible for this decision. Empty array when the transcript does not make it clear (default applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema).

confidence.description: Optional. Model confidence 0.0-1.0 that this is a real decision vs. discussion. Null or omitted when uncertain; any number outside [0.0, 1.0] fails the gate.

rationale.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+). The stated reason or justification WHY this decision was made, as expressed in the transcript — the explicit 'why', distinct from background decision context. Null or omitted when no rationale was stated; legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 decisions omit it entirely (additivity).

action_items.description: Additive: items may be a legacy string OR a structured object carrying the new optional `status` field. The string branch keeps every existing artifact and the LLM strict-schema eval (list[str]) passing.

status.description: Optional. Default "open" when absent (default applied by the producer, not enforced by JSON Schema). Only these three values are valid — e.g. "done" fails the gate.

follow_up_required.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+). Default true (applied by the producer, not enforced by JSON Schema): true when a human must take action before the next meeting, false for completed or informational items. Legacy action_item objects omit it (additivity).

open_questions.description: Additive: items may be a legacy string OR a structured Q&A-log object. The string branch preserves existing artifacts and the LLM strict-schema eval.

commitments.description: Optional, default []. Individual "I will do X" statements distinct from group action_items.

risks.description: Optional, default []. Things a speaker flagged as a potential problem.

severity.description: Optional severity. Null when the transcript does not state one. Any value outside low/medium/high (and null) fails the gate.

claims.description: Optional, default []. Factual or analytical assertions made in the meeting, distinct from `decisions` (which commit the group) and `risks` (flagged problems). Legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 artifacts omit this array entirely (additivity).

external_references.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default [] (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema). External documents, ITU articles, CFR sections, or other sources cited as evidence for this claim. E.g. ["ITU Article 21", "47 CFR 96.41", "OB3 Section 4.2"]. Empty array when none cited.

evidence_in_transcript.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default [] (applied by the producer/model). turn_ids that provide evidence SUPPORTING this claim — deliberately distinct from the PR #128 grounding/source_turns contract, which records where the claim was STATED. A claim may be stated in one turn but supported by evidence across multiple turns. This field does NOT alter or re-pin the grounding contract.

claim_complexity.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+). atomic = single independently verifiable fact; compound = bundles multiple facts that should be split. Default "atomic" (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema): the key is OPTIONAL so every legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 / 1.2.0 claim — and any claim that simply omits it — validates unchanged. Only these two values are valid; any other (e.g. "wrong") fails the gate.

cross_references.description: Optional, default []. References to other meetings or documents.

attendees.description: Optional, default []. Roster with name, agency, role.

present.description: Optional. Default true when absent (default applied by the producer).

regulatory_references.description: Optional, default []. OB3, statutory citations, rule references.

technical_parameters.description: Optional, default []. Exact numeric values (frequencies, bands, thresholds) stated verbatim.

named_artifacts.description: Optional, default []. Documents, folders, reports mentioned by name.

scheduled_events.description: Optional, default []. Future meetings/events with dates and purposes.

date.description: The event date as stated in the transcript. The key is required (the extraction model always emits it) but the value is null when no date was explicitly stated — a scheduled event mentioned without a date is still a real, faithful extraction, so null is valid and must not block (the model must never invent a date).

word_level_timestamps.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default false. Whether the chunks.jsonl for this transcript carries word-level timestamps. Set by the chunker at ingestion time, NEVER by the extraction model. False for the current docx inputs (no word-level timing data); infrastructure for future diarized transcripts. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

sentiment_indicators.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default []. Speaker turns flagged for notable sentiment. Only populated when a speaker expresses clear disagreement, concern, strong endorsement, uncertainty, or frustration — not routine discussion. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

sentiment.description: Exactly one of the five approved sentiment signals. Any other value (e.g. "annoyed") fails the gate.

meeting_phases.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default []. High-level phases of the meeting in sequence. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

phase_name.description: Exactly one of the five approved phase names. Any other value (e.g. "lunch") fails the gate.

issue_registry_entry.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. Substantive technical or policy problems being worked across multiple meetings; an issue persists until resolved. DISTINCT from `open_questions` (a procedural question raised in one meeting) and `decisions` (a resolution). Legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 / 1.2.0 artifacts omit it entirely (additivity — the array is optional, so absence validates).

issue_type.description: Exactly one of the five approved issue types. Any other value fails the gate.

status.description: Exactly one of the four approved statuses. Any other value (e.g. "done") fails the gate.

related_decisions.description: Optional, default []. Decision text or ids this issue is related to.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this entry.

position_statement.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. An agency's or participant's stated stance on a topic that may recur or evolve across meetings — a declared position, NOT a decision (a stance, not a resolution). Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

position_type.description: Exactly one of the five approved position types. Any other value fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this statement.

dissent_or_objection.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A formal disagreement explicitly registered on the record by a participant. DISTINCT from `risks` (a flagged potential problem) and `sentiment_indicators` (tone). Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

resolved.description: Optional. Default false (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema).

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this objection.

agenda_item.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. Formal agenda structure recoverable from the transcript (numbered or titled sections) — the organizational spine for cross-meeting document structure, NOT the substantive content (that stays in `topics` / `decisions`). Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

precedent_reference.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A speaker's reference to a prior meeting, prior decision, or prior study used as justification for a current position or direction. At scale these form a citation graph across the TIG process. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

purpose.description: Exactly one of the five approved purposes. Any other value fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this reference.

external_stakeholder_input.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. Input relayed from parties NOT present in the meeting (industry associations, ITU, congressional offices, OSD). Only when a speaker explicitly relays it — a bare document mention with no relayed content is NOT stakeholder input. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

input_type.description: Exactly one of the six approved input types. Any other value fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids where the input was relayed.

glossary_definition.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A term formally defined or clarified for the purpose of this study. At scale this builds a living domain glossary from the TIG process. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

authoritative.description: Optional. Default false (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema); true only when the speaker indicated this is the official/working definition for the study.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids where the term was defined.

procedural_ruling.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A chair / co-lead ruling on meeting procedure, scope boundaries, or process — DISTINCT from a `decisions` item (substantive). Establishes the governance framework. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

ruling_type.description: Exactly one of the six approved ruling types. Any other value fails the gate.

binding.description: Optional. Default true (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema).

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this ruling.

grounding.description: Phase Y (schema_version 1.1.0): one entry per extracted item with its source_turns. Shape left loose here so the additive change does not re-pin the Phase Y grounding contract.
